# UC-22.11.86 · Third-Party Service Provider Compliance Scorecard Ingest

## Description

Centralizes SOC reports and PCI AOC freshness dates for processors and cloud providers that affect account data so service-provider due diligence and monitoring obligations are continuously visible.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vendor and sourcetypes vendor:compliance_scorecard. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Automate monthly vendor portal scrape to HEC. (2) Map vendors to in-scope services in lookup. (3) Legal review when `days_to_pci<30`. (4) Attach artifacts in document store with Splunk link. Indexes required: `index=vendor`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vendor:compliance_scorecard`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard"` HEC from TPRM platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vendor` `sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard"` — `vendor`, `pci_aoc_expiry`, `soc2_expiry`, `open_findings`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Automate monthly vendor portal scrape to HEC. (2) Map vendors to in-scope services in lookup. (3) Legal review when `days_to_pci<30`. (4) Attach artifacts in document store with Splunk link.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vendor sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard" earliest=-1d
| eval pci_exp_epoch=strptime(pci_aoc_expiry,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_to_pci=round((pci_exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| where days_to_pci<60 OR open_findings>0
| table vendor, pci_aoc_expiry, days_to_pci, soc2_expiry, open_findings
| sort days_to_pci
```

Understanding this SPL

**Third-Party Service Provider Compliance Scorecard Ingest (PCI DSS Req 12.8.1, 12.8.2)** — Centralizes SOC reports and PCI AOC freshness dates for processors and cloud providers that affect account data so service-provider due diligence and monitoring obligations are continuously visible.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vendor` `sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard"` — `vendor`, `pci_aoc_expiry`, `soc2_expiry`, `open_findings`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard"` HEC from TPRM platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vendor; **sourcetype**: vendor:compliance_scorecard. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vendor, sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pci_exp_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_pci** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_to_pci<60 OR open_findings>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: days_to_pci, open_findings, pci_aoc_expiry, pci_exp_epoch, soc2_expiry, vendor.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (vendors at risk), timeline (expiry dates), single value (count expiring <60d).

## SPL

```spl
index=vendor sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard" earliest=-1d
| eval pci_exp_epoch=strptime(pci_aoc_expiry,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_to_pci=round((pci_exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| where days_to_pci<60 OR open_findings>0
| table vendor, pci_aoc_expiry, days_to_pci, soc2_expiry, open_findings
| sort days_to_pci
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (vendors at risk), timeline (expiry dates), single value (count expiring <60d).
